title: Post-order Traversal of Binary Tree using 1 stack

content.problemStatement: Given the root of a Binary Tree, write a recursive function that returns an array containing the postorder traversal of the tree.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 100
content.inputFormat: The input is the root of a binary tree where each node has an integer value.
content.outputFormat: The output is an array containing the postorder traversal of the binary tree.

solution.approach: Recursive approach to traverse the binary tree in postorder.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) where N is the number of nodes in the tree
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the recursive call stack and the output array

code.Python3:
# Tree node definition
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, left=None, right=None):
        self.val = val
        self.left = left
        self.right = right

# Solution class with postorder function
class Solution:
    def postorderTraversal(self, root, result):
        # Base case: if node is None
        if not root:
            return
        
        # Traverse left subtree
        self.postorderTraversal(root.left, result)
        
        # Traverse right subtree
        self.postorderTraversal(root.right, result)
        
        # Add current node value
        result.append(root.val)

# Main function
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Build tree:    1
    #              /   \
    #             2     3
    root = TreeNode(1)
    root.left = TreeNode(2)
    root.right = TreeNode(3)

    # Object of solution
    sol = Solution()
    result = []

    # Call postorder traversal
    sol.postorderTraversal(root, result)

    # Print result
    print(*result)
